diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with high blood pressure (SBP 230/120) and chest pain. He was previously hospitalized for a hypertensive emergency and exhibited similar symptoms upon return. The absence of acute cardiopulmonary findings on chest X-ray, a negative initial set of cardiac enzymes, and significant improvement of chest pain after morphine, along with escalating blood pressure management in the ED, supported the diagnosis of hypertensive emergency.
